qa.user_verbatim_complaint: bash_context_strip.py incorrectly strips quoted arguments from DANGER COMMANDS themselves (killall, pkill, kill, rm) — bypass confirmed: killall "happy" exits 0 instead of 2. 3 new tests (killall "happy", pkill -f "claude", kill "-9") must be registered in manifest active_tests[].

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 20260524-101700-A measured state: tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[] contains 19 entries (AC1-AC19, sequential, no gaps). Schema version 1.1 preserved. AC17 status=active type=hook expected_exit=2 command='killall "happy"'. AC18 status=active type=hook expected_exit=2 command='pkill -f "claude"'. AC19 status=active type=hook expected_exit=2 command='kill "-9" 1234'. pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/: 19 passed in 5.20s. pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py: 30 passed in 0.03s. Regression ACs AC3 and AC13 individually confirmed passing.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All three new manifest entries (AC17/AC18/AC19) are present with status=active, type=hook, expected_exit=2. pytest collects and runs all 19 tests, all pass. The quoted-arg bypass is confirmed blocked: test_AC17 (killall "happy" -> exit 2), test_AC18 (pkill -f "claude" -> exit 2), test_AC19 (kill "-9" 1234 -> exit 2). False-positive fix preserved: AC13 (codex exec with killall in LLM arg) still exits 0.

qa.overall_assessment: Task A PASS. Dev appended AC17, AC18, and AC19 to manifest.json active_tests[] exactly as specified. All 19 pytest tests pass (5.20s). All 30 unit tests in hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py pass (0.03s). Manifest entry fields for the three new entries match the AC schema precisely. No regressions detected. Provenance confirmed: manifest.json appears in git diff against baseline SHA 361e28c6.
